channel.title: nexusEVnews
channel.description: Independent EV and renewable energy news, curated daily. Electric vehicles, solar, battery tech, and charging infrastructure.

item.title: The Licensing Business Nobody Saw Coming
item.dc:creator: Jacob Hunka
item.description: Rivian's $5.8 billion deal with Volkswagen has been treated as a one-off. I don't think it is. I see it as the first proof point in a second business Rivian has been quietly building for years, and the next twelve months will show whether it works.

item.title: The R2 Is Rivian's Mass-Market Moment. A $5.8B Software Licensing Deal Hints at What Comes Next.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Technology
item.description: RJ Scaringe tells Bloomberg the R2 is in production with strong early demand. The lineup runs from $45,000 to $57,000, straddling the US average new car price. Point-to-point Level 2 autonomy arrives later in 2026; Level 3 follows in 2027. A second R2 variant at the end of 2026 adds Rivian's in-house 800-TOPS silicon and LiDAR. Scaringe also outlined Rivian's technology licensing strategy: the $5.8 billion VW deal covers Rivian's OS and compute stack, with autonomy licensing described as a longer-term target.

item.title: BMW's Updated 7 Series Has a New Face, 36 Speakers, and a Rear Screen That Streams YouTube
item.dc:creator: Autogefühl
item.description: Autogefühl does a full walkaround of the refreshed BMW 7 Series. The i7 gains 10 kWh for a 112.5 kWh pack, still on 400-volt architecture with 250 kW DC max and 29 minutes from 10 to 80 percent. The 740 xDrive runs a 3-liter inline-6 with 400 horsepower and 5.1 seconds to 100 km/h. The M760e PHEV tops out at 571 horsepower and 4.3 seconds; its PHEV battery is 18.7 kWh with AC charging only. The interior borrows from the Neue Klasse with a Panoramic Vision strip; the updated rear theater screen streams YouTube directly. Pricing starts at 120,000 euros.

item.title: Quebec Has About Half of Canada's EV Charging. A Tour of Autoroute 20 Shows Why.
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Roaming
item.description: Quebec hosts roughly half of all EV charging stations in Canada, powered by hydroelectricity at around 10 cents Canadian per kWh at home. Out of Spec Roaming drives an F-150 Lightning along Autoroute 20, covering Circuit Electric, Tesla, Parkland, Kushtar, and smaller regional networks. Circuit Electric pricing tiers to around 50 cents per kWh; Tesla is the cheapest option at about 47 cents ad hoc or 26 cents on subscription. The largest charging site in Canada is in St-Louis-de-Blandford with 44 chargers. Pull-through capability for trucks and trailers is now standard at provincial rest areas.

item.title: Smart's New City EV Is 2.97 Metres Long, Offers 300 km of Range, and Includes V2L
item.dc:creator: Everything Electric APAC
item.description: The Smart #2 is a compact four-seater on a purpose-designed platform called ECA, developed since 2019. It is 2.97 meters long and 1.9 meters wide, with a 6.95-meter turning circle. Claimed range is 300 km WLTP or 400 km CLTC, with V2L and DC fast charging included. Design was handled by Smart's European studio, reviving the two-tone body language of the original. The production-ready version debuts at Paris in October 2026, with sales expected early next year. A second vehicle shown, the Smart #6 sedan hybrid, is currently planned for China only.

item.title: Oil Prices Are Pushing Consumers Toward EVs. China Is Best Positioned to Benefit.
item.dc:creator: DW News
item.description: DW News examines how oil prices spiking from the Iran conflict are creating new urgency around EV adoption. Germany's EV registrations exceeded gasoline car sales for the first time in March. EU pump prices averaged €1.81 per liter in mid-April. In the US, EV demand dropped sharply after the federal tax credit was eliminated; VW sold just 250 ID4s in the three months after the credit ended and has paused Tennessee production of the model. China produces more than half of all EVs globally, controls over 80 percent of rare earth processing capacity, and Chinese brands hold over 80 percent of Brazil's EV market.

item.title: The 2027 Chevrolet Bolt Charges Three Times Faster Than Its Predecessor. Here's What the Data Shows.
item.dc:creator: State Of Charge
item.description: State Of Charge tests the 2027 Bolt RS at a 350 kW EVgo station. The LFP pack peaks at 158 kW, hits 70 percent in 30 minutes, and completes a full charge in under an hour. The original Bolt needed more than two hours for the same session. GM claims 26 minutes from 10 to 80 percent; this first test on a 22-mile-old battery showed 33.3 minutes, with a follow-up planned. EPA-rated range is 262 miles. Base price starts under $30,000.

item.title: A San Francisco Arcade Operator Fit a Skeeball Machine in the TELO Truck. Done.
item.dc:creator: TELO Trucks
item.description: Joey the Cat runs 40 Bay Area bar locations and moves about 50 skeeball machines and hundreds of arcade games between them. His test for the TELO electric truck: does a skeeball machine fit in the bed? It does, with room left for his tools. He currently hauls in a 2011 Toyota Tacoma and a 2021 Mercedes Sprinter van and wants a smaller electric option that can handle city parking without giving up cargo utility. Episode 8 of TELO's How Will You TELO? series.

item.title: CATL Unveiled Six Battery Technologies at Its 2026 Tech Day. The Headline Numbers Are Striking.
item.dc:creator: Everything Electric APAC
item.description: CATL's Super Technology Day brought the third-generation Shenxing battery at 10 to 80 percent in 3 minutes 44 seconds and 1,000 cycles with above 90 percent capacity retention. The Qilin Condensed Battery reaches 350 Wh/kg and 760 Wh/L for 1,500 km of range in a sedan. The third-generation Qilin LFP hits 280 Wh/kg and 3 MW peak power. The Naxtra sodium-ion battery is moving to large-scale production. CATL also announced 4,000 integrated charge-swap stations covering nearly 190 cities by end of 2026.

item.title: 13 Months and 38,700 Miles in a Used Taycan 4S: The Full Ownership Report
item.dc:creator: Driven Car Reviews
item.description: Tom Voelk bought a 2021 Taycan 4S CPO for $74,000 against a $163,000 MSRP. After 13 months: one software glitch that cleared with a five-minute lock-and-walk-away, several pre-purchase recalls already handled, and strong pack health. After a manual rebalance, indicated range is 215 miles in cold weather and expected to return to around 260 miles in summer. He charges overnight at 9 cents per kWh in Seattle. He would buy it again.

item.title: Subaru's New Uncharted EV Is Good Looking and Hard to Recommend
item.dc:creator: Auto Focus
item.description: Auto Focus reviews the Subaru Uncharted, a dual-motor AWD electric crossover with a 75 kWh battery, 338 hp, and roughly 250 miles of real-world range. The GT trim runs about $45,000; the base starts near $35,000. It shares its platform with Toyota's current EV, and the familiar trade-offs follow: a 150 kW charging ceiling and modest range make road trips a chore. The exterior design and composed ride quality are genuine positives. The Chevy Bolt EUV beats it on value at the low end; the Model Y covers the high end.

item.title: Mercedes Reveals the Electric C-Class With 330 kW Charging and 4-Second Acceleration
item.dc:creator: Autogefühl
item.description: Autogefühl covers the full reveal of the all-new electric Mercedes C-Class in Portugal. The C400 gets two motors, a two-speed transmission, and 0 to 100 km/h in 4 seconds. The 94.5 kWh net battery supports 330 kW DC peak charging, with 10 to 80 percent in 22 minutes. Pricing starts below 70,000 euros, with a fully specced car approaching 90,000 euros. The MBEA platform is shared with the GLC EQ. The BMW i3 counters with a larger 108.7 kWh battery; the C-Class answers with quicker acceleration and an optional air suspension plus rear-axle steering package.

item.title: Hyundai's IONIQ 3 Has 490 km of Range and a Boot That Hides an Entire Suitcase
item.dc:creator: Everything Electric CARS
item.description: Everything Electric CARS gets an early look at the Hyundai IONIQ 3 in Frankfurt. Two battery options: 42.2 kWh for 335 km, or 61 kWh for 490 km. Starting price is expected in the mid-£20,000s, with the N-Line top trim north of £30,000. UK deliveries are planned by end of summer 2026. The IONIQ 3 shares the EGMP platform with the Kia EV2 but its aerodynamic hatch shape adds 42 km of range on the long-range pack. A hidden lower boot compartment brings total cargo capacity to 441 liters.

item.title: Inside the Rivian R2: Upcycled Wood, a Linear Dash, and Design Built Around Adventure
item.dc:creator: Rivian
item.description: Rivian's official interior design walkthrough for the R2 covers the decisions behind the cabin. Nature is the stated inspiration. Upcycled birch from the furniture industry runs throughout, styled after mid-century modern furniture rather than conventional automotive trim. The linear dashboard layout is designed to make the interior feel wider than the vehicle's footprint. The design team describes the goal as balancing technology with the R2's physical capabilities across on-road, off-road, and storage use.

item.title: Australia Hit a Monthly EV Sales Record in March 2026. Charging Infrastructure Is the Next Problem.
item.dc:creator: A Current Affair
item.description: A Current Affair reports on Australia's EV market after March 2026 set a new record, with more sales that month than between 2011 and 2021 combined. Market share now sits at 14.8 percent, driven partly by rising fuel costs tied to Middle East oil supply tensions. The Tesla Model Y is Drive's 2026 Car of the Year. The BYD Shark electric ute is drawing interest from tradespeople. Industry analysts say charging infrastructure, particularly outside major cities, is now the primary barrier to broader adoption.

item.title: The 2025 Cadillac Optiq Starts at $56,000 and Drives Like It Costs More
item.dc:creator: Doug DeMuro
item.description: Doug DeMuro covers the 2025 Cadillac Optiq, a fully electric midsize SUV with around 300 miles of range, roughly 300 horsepower, and 0-60 in the mid-fives. It starts at about $56,000, well below rivals like the Polestar 3 and BMW iX despite a comparable real-world experience. Standard all-wheel drive, Super Cruise, and a rear-seat household outlet are included. The 500E4 badge on the tail denotes torque in Newton meters, not horsepower. Doug gives it 56 out of 100.

item.title: China Controls 70% of Global EV Production. Here's What That Looks Like Across Southeast Asia.
item.dc:creator: BBC World Service
item.description: The BBC World Service's Asia Specific podcast examines how China's EV dominance is reshaping the auto industry. According to the IEA, China accounts for more than 70 percent of global EV production, with more than half the cars sold in China now electric or hybrid. Chinese manufacturers are expanding factory operations into Thailand, Malaysia, and Indonesia; BYD was Singapore's top-selling car brand last year. The episode covers government policy, tariff tensions in Europe and the US, and why Chinese brands are pricing carefully abroad.

item.title: 574 Miles of WLTP Range, the Kia e-Niro's Last Chapter, and Tesla FSD Gets Dutch Approval
item.dc:creator: Electrifying
item.description: Electrifying's Kilowatt Half Hour covers the Mercedes EQS hitting 574 miles WLTP, with real-world bad-weather range still around 470 miles. Kia is discontinuing the e-Niro after 100,000 EV sales in the UK, with the EV3 and EV2 set to fill the gap. Tesla's Full Self-Driving Supervised system received approval from Dutch regulators after 18 months of road testing. Volkswagen previewed the ID Polo, ID Polo GTI, and ID3 Neo at a major Hamburg launch event.

item.title: The 2026 Renault Twingo Electric Adds 50 kW DC Fast Charging and Starts Under €21,500
item.dc:creator: JR Car Reviews
item.description: JR Car Reviews drives the 2026 Renault Twingo E-Tech Electric at the Slovenian factory where it is built alongside the Clio. The car runs a 60 kW motor producing 82 horsepower, paired with a 37.5 kWh LFP battery. WLTP range sits around 280 km. This generation adds 50 kW DC charging for the first time, alongside standard 6.5 kW AC. The top-spec version is priced at around €21,500. Front suspension components come from the R5; rear from the Captur. 0-100 km/h takes around 12.1 seconds.

item.title: An American in Shanghai on What BYD, NIO, XPeng, and Zeekr Are Really Building
item.dc:creator: MotorTrend Channel
item.description: MotorTrend interviews Ethan Robertson of Wheels, who is fluent in Mandarin and based in Shanghai, and has reviewed hundreds of Chinese EVs since 2019. He breaks down which brands have global staying power, where Tesla stands in the Chinese market, and why the domestic price war is forcing consolidation. His list for brands with international potential: BYD first, then Zeekr from Geely Group, followed by NIO, Xpeng, and Leauto. He also covers why a BYD Seagull-equivalent simply does not exist in the US market.
